narrativeDistance.whyNeeded: Bounds what the narrator may perceive to the focal character's perception, which is the same boundary the engine already enforces on facts; distance drift is how narrator omniscience and knowledge leaks begin.
narrativeDistance.overtighteningFailure: If distance is policed sentence-by-sentence, legitimate orientation sentences get flagged and scenes lose the ability to establish place at all.

tense.whyNeeded: A single declared tense prevents drift between scenes and matches the rehearsal-surface framing of the product; the value itself is a creator decision, not a quality claim.
tense.overtighteningFailure: None structural; the risk is choosing the wrong value for the product, which is why the alternative stays in allowedRange for creator review.

focalization.whyNeeded: One focal lane per scene is what makes knowledge-boundary validation meaningful; multi-focal prose would make private-knowledge screening undecidable.
focalization.changesWith: Fixed; a different focal character is a different request, not a lever change.
focalization.overtighteningFailure: Strict mode rejects harmless communal observations; if review shows this, the soft variant is the fallback, by creator decision.

actorNamingFrequency.whyNeeded: Naming policy is where epic pastiche enters English prose; banning epithets and fixing reference behavior removes the most common fake-Homeric marker without any taste judgment.
actorNamingFrequency.overtighteningFailure: Aggressive pronoun policy in scenes with two same-gender actors creates reference ambiguity; the until-ambiguity clause exists exactly for that case.

sentenceLengthDistribution.whyNeeded: Length distribution is the measurable half of rhythm; a ceiling on sentence length blocks the run-on accumulation typical of machine prose, and a short-sentence floor keeps pressure expressible.
sentenceLengthDistribution.changesWith: styleStateId (state overrides tighten the distribution under pressure states).
sentenceLengthDistribution.overtighteningFailure: Hard numeric enforcement produces chopped, metronomic prose; that is why this is a distribution target with heuristic ownership, not a per-sentence gate.

clauseComplexityCeiling.whyNeeded: Clause stacking is the structural form of translationese and of lecture-like narration; a ceiling keeps syntax concrete without prescribing any positive style.
clauseComplexityCeiling.overtighteningFailure: A ceiling of one would forbid ordinary cause-and-result sentences and force fragment-like output, which is itself a forbidden construction.

dialogueDensity.whyNeeded: Dialogue is the highest-invention-risk channel; the default of zero with licensed exceptions encodes the repaired rule that dialogue is never expected and never free.
dialogueDensity.changesWith: dialogueAuthority.mode in the preflight receipt; nothing else.
dialogueDensity.overtighteningFailure: If the ceiling stays at zero in practice because licenses are never issued, scenes with natural speakers will read as artificially mute; that is a creator policy signal, not a lever defect.

dialogueFunctionAllowlist.whyNeeded: Restricting speech to enumerated speech acts is what makes a line checkable: an act either changes information, action, relationship, or commitment, or it has no license to exist.
dialogueFunctionAllowlist.changesWith: Creator amendments to the allowlist only.
dialogueFunctionAllowlist.overtighteningFailure: Too small an allowlist forces every utterance into command/question shapes and flattens character interaction; amendments go through correction ingestion.

expositionBudget.whyNeeded: Uncapped orientation is where input recitation returns; a small budget forces the scene to spend words on sourced events rather than on restating the situation.
expositionBudget.changesWith: sceneMode (setup scenes may use the full budget; turn scenes should rarely need it).
expositionBudget.overtighteningFailure: A budget of zero makes scene openings unintelligible after a fork, because the reader loses place and time anchoring.

figurativeLanguageBudget.whyNeeded: Machine prose signals literariness through figuration density; one physically grounded figure at most keeps imagery available while structurally excluding the personified-abstraction opener and the speaking-body-part metaphor.
figurativeLanguageBudget.changesWith: styleStateId (pressure states may reduce to zero).
figurativeLanguageBudget.overtighteningFailure: Zero across all states produces affectless inventory prose, which then invites abstraction to carry feeling — the exact failure being prevented.

abstractionBudget.whyNeeded: Abstract-noun density is the strongest measurable correlate of the rejected analytic register; the epistemic-noun ban keeps narrator certainty-reporting out regardless of density.
abstractionBudget.overtighteningFailure: At zero, sentences about stakes become impossible to write without contortion; two per scene is the working ceiling pending creator review.

temporalOrderPolicy.whyNeeded: Chronological rendering is what keeps causality legible and machine-checkable against the event sequence; reordering is where invented causation hides.
temporalOrderPolicy.changesWith: Only a licensed recall detail (sensory_detail category) may interrupt order, hence the second allowed value exists for creator consideration.
temporalOrderPolicy.overtighteningFailure: Strict order forbids even licensed recollection; if the creator wants memory beats, the policy moves to chronological_with_licensed_recall rather than being silently ignored.

objectSpaceUsagePolicy.whyNeeded: Concreteness must come from registered anchors, not renderer-invented props; requiring one causal anchor use in turn scenes keeps objects doing work instead of decorating.
objectSpaceUsagePolicy.changesWith: sceneMode (the minimum applies to turn scenes only).
objectSpaceUsagePolicy.overtighteningFailure: Requiring anchor use in every mode forces setup and transition scenes to manufacture object business, which is fake activity — the same disease as fake change.

endingMode.whyNeeded: Each scene type has a different legitimate stopping shape; typing them prevents both reader-addressed handoffs and false closure on non-terminal scenes.
endingMode.overtighteningFailure: Treating the ending shape as deterministic would auto-reject legitimate variations; the shape check flags, the creator decides.

forbiddenConstructionIds.whyNeeded: Named, detectable constructions replace taste adjectives; each entry carries a structural detection criterion in the authority contract and a provenance (craft-reference failure mode or creator correction receipt).
forbiddenConstructionIds.changesWith: Creator corrections only (additive under lock, with receipt IDs).
forbiddenConstructionIds.overtighteningFailure: Over-broad detectors flag legitimate syntax; every FC entry therefore records its false-positive risk in the authority contract, and heuristics flag rather than fail.

translationRobustnessReview.whyNeeded: The original rejection surfaced through a translated rendering; an advisory pass asks whether each sentence's structure would survive literal translation without becoming a report, feeding a future Korean lane decision.
translationRobustnessReview.changesWith: Fixed until a Korean lane decision exists.
translationRobustnessReview.overtighteningFailure: Treating this as a gate would let a hypothetical translation veto English prose; it stays advisory and human-owned.

styleStates.trigger: Default engine state: no threat clock at or above its warning threshold, as mapped by the deterministic runtime to styleStateId.
Deterministic runtime maps a rising threat clock (at warning threshold) to this state.
Deterministic runtime maps a threat clock at its maximum, or a terminal compromised route, to this state.
